research.status_summary: Actively and aggressively hiring — OpenAI is targeting a near-doubling of headcount from ~4,500 to ~8,000 employees by end of 2026, with no layoffs reported internally.
research.hiring_policy: OpenAI sponsors H-1B visas (57 LCAs filed in FY2026, 100% approval rate) and provides immigration/relocation support; however, it is firmly hybrid/office-first (3 days in-office Mon–Wed), with only ~4% of open roles listed as remote and no EOR or work-from-anywhere policy — candidates outside a hub city are expected to relocate within ~45 days of hire.
recent_news: $122B funding round closed March 31, 2026 at an $852B valuation, anchored by Amazon ($50B), Nvidia ($30B), and SoftBank ($30B) — the largest private fundraise in history; an IPO is widely expected by Q4 2026 or Q1 2027.

Product velocity in overdrive: GPT-5.5 released April 23 and set as ChatGPT default in May; Sora 2 (flagship video/audio generation) launched as a standalone product; Codex (coding agent) doubled API revenue growth rate within one week of its Goal Mode launch in May 2026.

Disney signed a landmark 3-year licensing deal giving Sora access to 200+ characters across Disney, Marvel, Pixar, and Star Wars for user-prompted social videos; OpenAI also launched 'Rosalind Biodefense' (GPT-Rosalind for life-sciences AI) in early June 2026 — signaling aggressive vertical expansion beyond consumer chat.
research.growth_signal: Revenue run-rate reached ~$2B/month (~$24B annualized), enterprise now constitutes 40% of revenue (up from 30% a year ago) and is on track to reach parity with consumer by end of 2026, while headcount is set to nearly double — the clearest hyper-growth signal in the current AI landscape.
research.fit_for_lior: OpenAI is a direct high-priority target for Lior: Sora 2, ChatGPT Images, and the Disney creative-IP deal sit squarely in her Creative AI thread, Codex + GPT-5.5 power her Vibe Coder angle, and the explosive enterprise + product analytics surface (900M weekly users, ads pilot, agentic workflows) maps perfectly onto a Product Analyst / AI Product role — especially given OpenAI's stated push to build out product development and 'technical ambassadorship' headcount.
